repo_notes.content: juke.lute is a personal, non-commercial LOTRO (Lord of the Rings Online) music library for solo lute players. It is NOT a traditional software application: the primary artifact is curated ABC notation files (format v2.11) packaged as ZIP archives, plus a small set of PowerShell and Python helper scripts. All ABC files are manually edited for solo in-game play through a process the author calls 'de-enshittification' (removing multi-part arrangements, simplifying notation, staying under LOTRO's ~150KB file limit). There is no CI/CD, no automated build pipeline, and no web application — every script is run manually by the developer. License: MIT. Target audience: solo instrument players who browse melodies via a Songbook MOD rather than typing /play commands.
repo_notes.author: awuehler

repo_notes.content: Repository layout has two top-level directories. (1) 999.songs/ — distribution archives and archive documentation. Contains juke.lute.zip (largest and most important asset, ~30MB, primary lute collection), plus secondary instrument archives: juke.fiddle.zip, juke.duet.zip, juke.flute.zip, juke.violin.zip, juke.bassoon.zip. Also contains README.md (download and LOTRO deployment instructions) and notes_folders.md (folder taxonomy). (2) my.tools/ — automation scripts and tool documentation. Contains jukebox.ps1 (random ABC playback tester with skip/quit and process-tree cleanup), zip_loop.ps1 (archive packager with -MusicPath/-Destination/-Compression/-WhatIf), songbook.ps1 (PowerShell Songbook index builder; preferred), songbook.hta (original Chiran HTA reference), web_get.py (HTTP file server), web_env.py (server config), README.md, notes_scripts.md (usage examples including Songbook Index), and notes_playlists.md. Root README.md provides project introduction and caveats.
repo_notes.author: awuehler

repo_notes.content: Content organization philosophy: 'play by ear' self-organizing buckets, NOT strict hierarchical taxonomy. Files may appear in multiple folders when they fit multiple moods or durations. Filenames use long descriptive format: <Artist_Name>-<Song_Name>(instrument).abc — intentionally incompatible with LOTRO /play command-line shortcuts, optimized for human readability in Songbook MOD panels. Chiran Songbook (stable, unmaintained, larger panel) or Badger SongbookBB (actively maintained) is mandatory. After extracting archives to C:\Users\<user>\Documents\The Lord of the Rings Online\Music\juke.lute, users must rebuild the ABC index with songbook.ps1 (preferred PowerShell port) or the original songbook.hta.
repo_notes.author: awuehler

repo_notes.content: Key script relationships and data flow: (A) Development loop — edit ABC files in the LOTRO Music folder; test with jukebox.ps1 (params: -MusicPath, -PlayerPath, -EditorPath, -PauseSeconds, -History). jukebox scans *.abc paths as strings, builds a lowercase folder menu (shared leaves like favs, leading instruments bassoon/flute/violin, then other instrument paths with juke. prefix stripped), filters via GetFolderMelodyPool, picks via ProbabilityPick against $script:MelodyTrack, parses T: title+duration via GetAbcTitleInfo, launches AbcPlayer/Maestro through PlayMelody with unique TEMP redirect logs, waits via WaitMelodyInterval (any key=skip, Q/Escape=quit), and always stops the player process tree in a try/finally. (B) Packaging loop — zip_loop.ps1 uses $PSScriptRoot (runnable from anywhere), scans juke* folders under Music (default Documents path), purges only matching juke*.zip under 999.songs/, then Compress-Archive. Supports -MusicPath, -Destination, -Compression, -WhatIf. (C) Index loop — songbook.ps1 (or songbook.hta) writes SongbookData.plugindata under PluginData\<user>\AllServers\; songbook.ps1 adds -JukeOnly, -AllUsers, -WhatIf, -UserName, -MusicPath, -LotroHome. (D) Distribution loop — web_get.py serves ABC/MIDI over HTTP using web_env.py. (E) In-game loop — download ZIP → extract to Music → rebuild Songbook index → play via MOD.
repo_notes.author: awuehler

repo_notes.content: ABC acquisition pipeline (documented in my.tools/README.md, not automated in repo): LIVE recording → AUDIO (MP3/WAV/FLAC via FFmpeg) → MIDI (via WaoN or manual) → ABC (via Maestro from github.com/digero/maestro) → manual editing/de-enshittification → jukebox.ps1 testing → zip_loop.ps1 packaging → git push. Playlist merging (notes_playlists.md): combine multiple melodies into one .abc file with matching M:/Q:/K: fields and invisible rest (x24) separators; max ~150KB for LOTRO. Future aspirational goal: train an LLM on the jukebox corpus for AI-generated ABC (see references to gwern.net/gpt-2-music, bgpt, folkrnn.org in my.tools/README.md).
repo_notes.author: awuehler

repo_notes.content: Documentation priorities for wiki generation: Use the explicit 'pages' array in this file as the definitive wiki structure — do not substitute auto-generated page titles. Highest-priority components: juke.lute.zip, my.tools/jukebox.ps1, and my.tools/songbook.ps1 (index rebuild after every extract). Also document zip_loop.ps1 packaging and songbook.hta as the unmodified Chiran reference. Emphasize dual consumption paths: production (Songbook MOD in LOTRO) vs development (jukebox.ps1 + AbcPlayer/Maestro). Include the manual-only workflow — no automation triggers on commit. Reference notes_scripts.md for console examples (Jukebox Player, ZIP Creator, Songbook Index, Localhost Webserver). External dependencies not in repo: Maestro/AbcPlayer, EasyABC, Notepad++/VSCodium, FFmpeg, WaoN, PowerShell 7, Python 3.x.
repo_notes.author: awuehler

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce the juke.lute repository, explaining its purpose as a collection of ABC music files for LOTRO solo lute play, and providing a high-level overview of the archive collections and tooling ecosystem

page_notes.content: Primary sources: README.md (project intro, caveats, author background), 999.songs/README.md (jukebox metaphor), my.tools/README.md (tooling overview), my.tools/notes_scripts.md (script examples). Include a high-level architecture diagram showing 999.songs/ and my.tools/ and the five workflows: edit → jukebox test → zip_loop package → songbook index → deploy/share. Emphasize this is a music content repository with helper scripts, not an application framework. Mention MIT license, private non-commercial use, ABC notation v2.11, and the DeepWiki badge link in README.md. Note PowerShell tools: jukebox.ps1, zip_loop.ps1, songbook.ps1; Python: web_get.py/web_env.py; reference HTA: songbook.hta.

pages.title: Music Archive Collections
pages.purpose: Document the instrument-specific ZIP archives that contain ABC music files, emphasizing the critical juke.lute.zip archive and explaining the archive format and deployment lifecycle

page_notes.content: Focus on 999.songs/ directory. List all ZIP archives: juke.lute.zip (~30MB, primary), juke.fiddle.zip, juke.duet.zip, juke.flute.zip, juke.violin.zip, juke.bassoon.zip with approximate sizes from notes_scripts.md examples. Explain each ZIP contains a folder hierarchy of *.abc files organized by mood/duration buckets. Document the archive lifecycle: edited in LOTRO Music folder → packaged by zip_loop.ps1 → stored in 999.songs/ → downloaded by users → extracted back to LOTRO Music folder → indexed by songbook.ps1 (or songbook.hta). Reference 999.songs/README.md for download instructions and notes_folders.md for internal folder taxonomy.

pages.title: Lute Collection (Primary Archive)
pages.purpose: Detailed documentation of juke.lute.zip, the most critical archive (importance: 91.17), including its internal architecture, deployment pipeline, data flow, and integration patterns

page_notes.content: juke.lute.zip is the dominant asset (importance score 91.17 in prior DeepWiki analysis). Document internal folder structure from notes_folders.md: folk, formal, jams, long2, long4, long6, quick, quiet, rural, sadly, show, spirit, sideb, older (on-disk paths under juke.lute; jukebox.ps1 displays them as lute\folk after stripping the juke. prefix). Show example filenames from 999.songs/README.md (e.g. Led_Zeppelin-Stairway_To_Heaven(lute).abc). Explain deployment target path: C:\Users\<user>\Documents\The Lord of the Rings Online\Music\juke.lute\. Note that ABC files inside the ZIP are the actual deliverable — the repo stores compressed binaries, not loose .abc source files. Include a data-flow diagram from zip_loop.ps1 Music source path to 999.songs/juke.lute.zip to LOTRO Music folder to songbook.ps1 PluginData index.

pages.title: Additional Instrument Archives
pages.purpose: Document the secondary instrument archives (fiddle, duet, flute, violin, bassoon), their structure, and how they complement the primary lute collection

page_notes.content: Cover juke.fiddle.zip, juke.duet.zip, juke.flute.zip, juke.violin.zip, juke.bassoon.zip in 999.songs/. These are secondary collections tuned for other LOTRO instruments (fiddle, bassoon-lute duets, flute, violin). Filename convention appends instrument tag: (fiddle), (duet), (flute), (violin), (bassoon), (lute). jukebox.ps1 folder menu (notes_scripts.md) shows lowercase display labels such as favs, bassoon, flute, violin, lute\folk, duet\bassoon-lute — shared leaf favs across instruments, leading instrument roots first, then remaining paths. Explain these archives share the same bucket organization philosophy but target different in-game instrument combinations. Sizes vary widely: juke.flute.zip (~246KB) vs juke.duet.zip (~7MB).

pages.title: Content Organization Philosophy
pages.purpose: Explain the 'play by ear' organization system, folder structure (folk, formal, long6, etc.), file naming conventions, and the anti-hierarchical design philosophy

page_notes.content: Primary source: 999.songs/README.md ('play by ear' section) and 999.songs/notes_folders.md. Key concepts: (1) self-organizing buckets, not strict hierarchy; (2) files may exist in multiple folders; (3) jazz-improvisation metaphor for session organization; (4) filename format <Artist_Name>-<Song_Name>(instrument).abc with long descriptive names; (5) NO /play command-line support by design — Songbook MOD required; (6) 'Traditional' prefix when composer unknown; (7) folder purposes: folk=traditional, formal=classical, long6=6+ minutes, quick=fast tempo, quiet=quiet locations, sadly=heartfelt, show=movies/shows, spirit=holidays, sideb=needs edits, jams=playlists. Include the folder listing table from notes_folders.md. Note jukebox display paths strip the juke. prefix (juke.lute\folk → lute\folk).

pages.title: Jukebox Playback System
pages.purpose: Document the jukebox.ps1 random player system for continuous ABC file playback, including architecture, player selection, skip/quit controls, and usage patterns

page_notes.content: Primary source: my.tools/jukebox.ps1 and my.tools/notes_scripts.md (Jukebox Player section). Purpose: random continuous ABC playback for debugging and enjoyment outside LOTRO. Architecture: optional CLI overrides → player prompt (1=AbcPlayer, 2=Maestro) → validate chosen exe → scan Music folder for *.abc FullName strings → GetMusicFolderList menu → GetFolderMelodyPool pre-filter → seed $script:MelodyTrack → try/finally play loop. Each iteration: NextMelody (ProbabilityPick + GetAbcTitleInfo) → display Title/Playtime/Wait/Selection → PlayMelody → WaitMelodyInterval. Controls: any key skips (stops player tree), Q or Escape quits; finally always calls StopJukeboxPlayer. Key helpers: ConvertFrom/ToJukeRelativePath, GetFolderMelodyPool, GetMusicFolderList, StopProcessTree, StopMaestroJavaChildren, RemovePlayerLogFiles/ClearStalePlayerLogs, PlayMelody, GetAbcTitleInfo, ConvertDurationToSeconds, ReadMelodyControlKey, WaitMelodyInterval. Include an execution flow diagram from launch through folder pick to skip/quit.

pages.title: Jukebox Player Configuration
pages.purpose: Detailed explanation of jukebox.ps1 parameters, End-User Modifications defaults, folder menu rules, history tracking, and title/duration parsing

page_notes.content: CLI parameters (CmdletBinding): -MusicPath, -PlayerPath, -EditorPath, -PauseSeconds (0–3600; -1=default), -History (0–100; -1=default). End-User Modifications defaults: $music_abc_history=3, $music_abc_pause=3, $music_abc_fallback='0:55', Documents-based $music_abc_path, default Maestro install paths for AbcPlayer/Maestro, $music_shared_folders=@('favs'), $music_leading_instruments=@('bassoon','flute','violin'). Folder menu: lowercase; shared allowlisted leaves that exist under any juke.*; then leading bare instrument roots; then remaining instrument-relative paths with juke. stripped; plus 'all'. History: $script:MelodyTrack (not global); InitializeMelodyHistory seeds the queue; ProbabilityPick prefers paths not in recent history. GetAbcTitleInfo reads the first T: line for title text and optional (mm:ss); ConvertDurationToSeconds for track length; Wait = track + pause (displayed separately as Playtime vs Wait). Paths unquoted once via GetUnquotedPath. Reference PowerShell execution policy notes in script .NOTES and the console example in notes_scripts.md.

pages.title: External Player Integration
pages.purpose: Document integration with AbcPlayer.exe and Maestro.exe, including process lifecycle, stdout redirect logs, and cleanup on skip/quit

page_notes.content: Both players come from the Maestro project (github.com/digero/maestro). Default install path: C:\Program Files (x86)\Maestro\. PlayMelody receives validated -PlayerExePath and -AbcPath (structured NextMelody object: Path, Title, Duration). Start-Process redirects stdout/stderr to unique %TEMP%\jukebox-player-<stamp>-<guid>.{stdout,stderr}.log files (avoids relaunch conflicts); StopJukeboxPlayer deletes tracked logs and ClearStalePlayerLogs prunes older jukebox-player-*.log files. StopProcessTree recursively kills the launcher and children; StopMaestroJavaChildren also stops java/javaw whose ExecutablePath is under the Maestro install directory (WinRun4J often leaves a JVM behind). try/finally ensures cleanup on quit, error, or interrupt. Option 1: AbcPlayer.exe (playback). Option 2: Maestro.exe (editor+player). In-game playback remains LOTRO /music via Songbook MOD — jukebox is for out-of-game testing.

pages.title: ABC File Management Workflow
pages.purpose: Comprehensive guide to creating, converting, editing, and managing ABC music files, from source acquisition through MIDI conversion to final ABC format

page_notes.content: Parent page for the full ABC content lifecycle. End-to-end diagram: LIVE → AUDIO → MIDI → ABC → edit/de-enshittify → jukebox.ps1 test → zip_loop.ps1 package → git push → user download → extract → songbook.ps1 index → LOTRO play. Primary sources: my.tools/README.md (ABC, MIDI, AUDIO, LIVE), notes_playlists.md, notes_scripts.md. Emphasize every conversion step is manual — no automation scripts exist for LIVE/AUDIO/MIDI conversion. Key constraints: ABC v2.11, ~150KB LOTRO file size limit, solo lute optimization, T: field should include (mm:ss) for accurate jukebox timing (fallback 0:55 when missing). Cross-reference child pages for zip_loop.ps1, acquisition pipeline, playlist merging, and editing tools.

pages.title: Archive Creation with zip_loop.ps1
pages.purpose: Document the automated ZIP archive creation process, including parameters, folder scanning, purge rules, compression, and the deployment workflow

page_notes.content: Primary source: my.tools/zip_loop.ps1 and notes_scripts.md (ZIP Creator section). Runnable from any directory via $PSScriptRoot (repo root = parent of my.tools). Parameters: -MusicPath (default Documents\...\Music), -Destination (default <repo>\999.songs), -Compression Fastest|Optimal|NoCompression (default Fastest), -WhatIf (SupportsShouldProcess). Flow: ShowJukeArchives BEFORE → RemoveJukeArchives (only juke*.zip, -Force) → NewJukeArchives (Compress-Archive per juke* Music folder) → ShowJukeArchives AFTER. Does not depend on relative ..\\Documents paths. Include before/after listing example from notes_scripts.md. Output names: juke.<instrument>.zip matching each source folder name.

pages.title: ABC File Acquisition and Conversion
pages.purpose: Explain the complete pipeline from live recordings and audio files through MIDI conversion using Maestro, WaoN, and FFmpeg to final ABC format

page_notes.content: Primary source: my.tools/README.md sections LIVE, AUDIO, MIDI, ABC. Pipeline stages (all manual, out-of-repo tooling): (1) LIVE: capture stream/session in media format; (2) AUDIO: convert to MP3/WAV/FLAC using FFmpeg; (3) MIDI: transcribe audio to MIDI using WaoN or find existing MIDI from sites like FreeMIDI; (4) ABC: convert MIDI to ABC using Maestro application; (5) EDIT: manual de-enshittification in EasyABC or text editor. External ABC libraries for reference: abcnotation.com, trillian.mit.edu/~jc/cgi/abc/tunefind, kunstderfuge.com. Note 'Fat Lute' archives as a common LOTRO ABC source. Audio-to-MIDI and live-to-audio steps are explicitly out of scope — left as reader exercises per README.md.

pages.title: Playlist Creation and Merging
pages.purpose: Document the process of creating ABC playlists by merging multiple melody files, including M:Q:K: field matching requirements and invisible rest notation

page_notes.content: Primary source: my.tools/notes_playlists.md. Key rules: (1) all merged tunes must share matching M: (meter), Q: (tempo), K: (key) fields; (2) only one X:, T:, C:, Z:, M:, Q:, K: header block per playlist file — subsequent tunes comment out their headers with %; (3) use invisible rest x24 between tunes (24 beats = ~12 seconds at 4/4, Q:122); (4) max file size ~150KB for LOTRO playability; (5) T: title field has 60-character LOTRO limit. Example playlist structure in notes_playlists.md shows 3-melody rural blues medley. The 'jams' folder in juke.lute contains playlist *.abc files. Editing must use plain text editors (Notepad++, not word processors).

pages.title: Editing Tools and Resources
pages.purpose: Reference guide for desktop applications (Notepad++, VSCodium, EasyABC), scripting environments (PowerShell, Python, Bash), and external ABC/MIDI libraries

page_notes.content: Primary source: my.tools/README.md (Applications, ABC, MIDI, Resources sections). Desktop tools: Notepad++ / VSCodium (text editing), EasyABC (ABC visualization/editing), Maestro/AbcPlayer (MIDI conversion and playback), VLC (media). Scripting: PowerShell 7 (jukebox.ps1, zip_loop.ps1, songbook.ps1), Python 3 (web_get.py), Bash via WSL (cross-platform file operations). ABC resources: abcnotation.com, trillian.mit.edu abc tunefind, freesheetmusic.net directory, kunstderfuge.com. MIDI resources: FreeMIDI, MIDI World, Bit MIDI, etc. Preprocessing: FFmpeg (audio), WaoN (audio-to-MIDI). Note many ABC/MIDI websites are outdated — WayBack Machine recommended. Chiran_MOD-02.png in my.tools/ shows Songbook MOD settings screenshot.

pages.title: Local Distribution and Web Sharing
pages.purpose: Document the HTTP server system for sharing ABC files over local networks using web_get.py and web_env.py configuration

page_notes.content: Primary source: my.tools/web_get.py, my.tools/web_env.py, notes_scripts.md (Localhost Webserver section). Purpose: ad-hoc redistribution of ABC/MIDI files over local network via HTTP GET. Typical usage: cd to LOTRO Music folder, run 'python web_get.py', share printed URLs with clients on same LAN. Supports browser download, wget, and curl. WSL example in notes_scripts.md shows wget mirror command with rate limiting. Server binds to web_env.LOCAL_HOST (0.0.0.0) on web_env.LOCAL_PORT (8000). Not intended for public internet exposure — localhost/LAN only. Cross-reference child pages for web_get.py internals and web_env.py settings.

pages.title: HTTP File Server (web_get.py)
pages.purpose: Detailed documentation of the Python HTTP server script, including IP discovery, file scanning, URL generation, and Python 2/3 compatibility

page_notes.content: Primary source: my.tools/web_get.py. Functions: host_ip_sock() discovers routable IP via UDP socket connect to 10.0.0.0; walk_web_file() os.walk('.') from CWD, filters by web_env.TARGET_EXTENSIONS, builds web_env.SERVE_FILES list, prints fully qualified HTTP URLs; warn_ip_address() flags non-routable IPs; main() sets up TCPServer with SimpleHTTPRequestHandler (Py2) or http.server.SimpleHTTPRequestHandler (Py3), calls serve_forever(). Python 2/3 compatibility via sys.version_info check at top. Must be started from the directory containing files to serve (typically LOTRO Music folder). Exit via CTRL-C triggers httpd.shutdown(). Known limitation: no upper limit on files printed to console (noted in TODO).

pages.title: Server Configuration (web_env.py)
pages.purpose: Document the web server configuration file, including LOCAL_HOST, LOCAL_PORT, TARGET_EXTENSIONS, and SERVE_FILES settings

page_notes.content: Primary source: my.tools/web_env.py (entire file is ~12 lines). Settings: LOCAL_HOST='0.0.0.0' (bind all interfaces), LOCAL_PORT='8000' (HTTP port as string, cast to int in web_get.py), TARGET_EXTENSIONS=['.abc', '.mid'] (file types to index and serve), SERVE_FILES=[] (populated at runtime by walk_web_file()). To serve additional file types, add extensions to TARGET_EXTENSIONS. To change port, edit LOCAL_PORT (ensure firewall allows it). SERVE_FILES is a runtime accumulator — not pre-configured. Imported by web_get.py via 'import web_env'. No authentication or access control — designed for trusted local networks only.

pages.title: LOTRO Game Integration
pages.purpose: Explain how ABC archives integrate with Lord of the Rings Online, including the mandatory Songbook MOD requirement and deployment to the LOTRO Music folder

page_notes.content: Primary sources: 999.songs/README.md, my.tools/README.md (Modifications section), my.tools/notes_scripts.md (Songbook Index). LOTRO supports ABC notation v2.11 via in-game /music. Long filenames make /play impractical — Songbook MOD is mandatory. MOD options: Chiran Songbook (stable, unmaintained, larger panel — preferred) and Badger SongbookBB (active at linawillow.org). Deploy ABC files to Documents\The Lord of the Rings Online\Music\. After every archive update, rebuild the index with songbook.ps1 (preferred) or original songbook.hta. Diagram: GitHub ZIP → extract → Music\juke.* → songbook.ps1 → PluginData\<user>\AllServers\SongbookData.plugindata → in-game MOD panel → /music playback.

pages.title: Deployment to LOTRO Music Folder
pages.purpose: Step-by-step guide for extracting archives, organizing files in the LOTRO Music directory, rebuilding the Songbook index, and the manual update workflow

page_notes.content: Primary source: 999.songs/README.md (Download the jukebox section) and notes_scripts.md (Songbook Index). Steps: (1) Download latest juke.<instrument>.zip from GitHub 999.songs/; (2) Remove or rename previous juke folder in LOTRO Music directory; (3) Extract ZIP to create new juke.<instrument> folder; (4) Run .\songbook.ps1 from my.tools (or songbook.hta) to rebuild SongbookData.plugindata; (5) Launch LOTRO, enable Songbook MOD, browse and play. Optional: songbook.ps1 -JukeOnly for jukebox-only index; -AllUsers to update every local LOTRO account; -WhatIf to preview counts. Update workflow repeats steps 1–4 for each new release. Warning: always remove previous juke folders before extracting new ZIP to avoid stale files. No automated update mechanism.

pages.title: Songbook Index Builder
pages.purpose: Document songbook.ps1 and the reference songbook.hta tool used to generate SongbookData.plugindata for the Chiran Songbook MOD

page_notes.content: Primary sources: my.tools/songbook.ps1, my.tools/songbook.hta (unmodified Chiran HTA reference with attribution comments), notes_scripts.md (Songbook Index section). songbook.ps1 is a PowerShell port that preserves Chiran SongbookData.plugindata format while adding parameters: -UserName (LOTRO login / PluginData folder; prompted if omitted), -MusicPath, -LotroHome (when Documents is relocated), -JukeOnly (index only juke.* trees), -AllUsers (write plugindata for every account found via UserPreferences.ini / PluginData; cannot combine with -UserName), -WhatIf (scan/report without writing). Scans *.abc and *.txt under Music (same extensions as HTA). Output: Documents\The Lord of the Rings Online\PluginData\<user>\AllServers\SongbookData.plugindata. Prefer songbook.ps1 for day-to-day rebuilds; keep songbook.hta as the vendor-format reference. Example console output and -JukeOnly/-AllUsers usage are in notes_scripts.md.

pages.title: Songbook MOD Requirements
pages.purpose: Explain why the Songbook MOD (Chiran or Badger) is mandatory due to long filenames, document MOD selection criteria, and explain the index rebuild process

page_notes.content: Primary sources: 999.songs/README.md (NO in-game command-line support section), my.tools/README.md (Modifications section), my.tools/Chiran_MOD-02.png, my.tools/songbook.ps1. Why mandatory: filenames like Led_Zeppelin-Stairway_To_Heaven(lute).abc are too long for /play typing — designed for MOD panel browsing. MOD comparison: Chiran Songbook — older, unmaintained, popular, stable, larger panel (screenshot in repo); Badger SongbookBB — actively developed at linawillow.org. Index rebuild: songbook.ps1 (or songbook.hta) scans Music recursively and writes SongbookData.plugindata. Must rerun after every ZIP extract/update. MOD installation steps left as reader exercise per README.md.

pages.title: Script Reference
pages.purpose: Consolidated reference documentation for all PowerShell and Python scripts in the my.tools/ directory

page_notes.content: Catalog my.tools/ scripts: jukebox.ps1 (random ABC player with CLI overrides, folder menu, skip/quit, process-tree stop), zip_loop.ps1 (ZIP archive creator; $PSScriptRoot-based), songbook.ps1 (SongbookData.plugindata index builder; preferred), songbook.hta (original Chiran HTA reference), web_get.py (HTTP file server), web_env.py (server config). Supporting docs: README.md, notes_scripts.md (Jukebox / Webserver / ZIP / Songbook examples), notes_playlists.md. Scripts are standalone — no shared modules. PowerShell targets PS 7+ (PS 3/5 generally work). Python web_get.py supports Py2 and Py3. Summary table: Script | Language | Purpose | Key params | Key functions/entrypoints.

pages.title: Script Usage Guide
pages.purpose: Quick reference for executing jukebox.ps1, zip_loop.ps1, songbook.ps1, and web_get.py, including parameters, prerequisites, and common workflows

page_notes.content: Primary source: my.tools/notes_scripts.md (all four example sections). jukebox.ps1: run from any directory; optional -MusicPath/-PlayerPath/-EditorPath/-PauseSeconds/-History; prompts for player and folder; any key=skip, Q/Escape=stop. zip_loop.ps1: run from any directory; -MusicPath/-Destination/-Compression/-WhatIf; prints BEFORE/AFTER listings. songbook.ps1: .\songbook.ps1 [-UserName] [-MusicPath] [-LotroHome] [-JukeOnly] [-AllUsers] [-WhatIf]. web_get.py: run from target directory ('python web_get.py'); prints URLs; CTRL-C stops server. Common workflow: edit ABCs → jukebox.ps1 test → zip_loop.ps1 package → git commit/push → songbook.ps1 after local extract → optional web_get.py LAN share.

pages.title: Development Environment Setup
pages.purpose: Guide to setting up the development environment, including PowerShell execution policies, Python version requirements, required applications, and workspace configuration

page_notes.content: Prerequisites from script .NOTES and README.md: PowerShell 7+ with Unrestricted execution policy (Set-ExecutionPolicy Unrestricted as admin), Python 3.x (2.x also supported for web_get.py), Maestro/AbcPlayer at default path (or pass -PlayerPath/-EditorPath), LOTRO Music folder at Documents default (or -MusicPath), at least one juke.* tree extracted for jukebox/zip_loop/songbook testing. Optional: WSL, EasyABC, Notepad++/VSCodium, FFmpeg, WaoN. Workspace: git clone git@github.com:awuehler/juke.lute.git. .gitignore excludes IDE/cache/binary noise. No package manager dependencies (no requirements.txt, no package.json).

pages.title: Future Development and AI Integration
pages.purpose: Document the aspirational goals for AI-driven ABC music generation, including planned LLM training using the jukebox corpus and remaining near-term script backlog

page_notes.content: Primary source: my.tools/README.md (AI section) and in-script TODO comments. Aspirational AI goal (no AI code in repo yet): train an LLM on the jukebox ABC corpus (Hugging Face); references gwern.net/gpt-2-music, sander-wood/bgpt, folkrnn.org, themachinefolksession.org. Near-term jukebox.ps1 backlog still open: metadata-based pick (keys/beats/keywords), restart after N iterations, optional second-folder prompt. Completed since earlier wiki text: skip key (any key), Q/Escape quit, CLI path/pause/history overrides, process-tree stop, unique TEMP logs with cleanup, structured Title/Duration display, songbook.ps1 index port. zip_loop.ps1 formerly CWD-bound Music path TODOs are largely addressed via -MusicPath/-Destination/$PSScriptRoot. web_get.py TODO remains: upper limit on console file listing.
